finding_results.notes: Three Damian turns in the basement scene reworked with on-page pressure tells in the dialogue itself. (1) Line 119: the Mertens analysis now breaks mid-sentence ('He thinks the SSS is going to —') with a vocal-register drop and a blunt admission replacing the polished aphorism 'Fear is narrowing his imagination.' (2) Line 133: the organized 'So. Fact, fact, fact' briefing framing replaced with a broken restart ('Both before sunrise, and I keep — / I keep trying to make that two separate things and I can't'); the follow-up exchange shifts from the analytical 'A very good one' to the more pressured 'It doesn't feel like a guess,' and 'Yes' becomes 'Yeah' as the diplomatic register slips. (3) Line 229: the four-sentence stacked situation-report interrupted mid-thought ('The mole is still —'), with a physical break and the diplomatic conclusion replaced by raw personal concern about Mertens's children. The replanning section (lines 237–253) was left relatively composed, which reads as Damian reassembling himself into operational mode after the pressure cracks — a character-true progression rather than uniform strain.

summary: Single finding addressed across three Damian turns in the basement scene. Each revised turn now carries a pressure tell inside the dialogue itself — interrupted sentences, broken analytical framing, register collapse from diplomatic to blunt — rather than delivering operational information as polished mini-briefings. The later replanning exchange was preserved at a cleaner register to allow a dramatic arc from cracked composure to operational recovery. No structural, continuity, or heading-contract changes required.
